commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix raw transport reads to use the existing 64 MiB SDK response cap when callers omit `maxBytes`, and adds focused transport tests for over-cap and under-cap raw responses.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +63. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main and v2026.6.10 route raw Matrix requests with omitted `maxBytes` to `Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer())`, so an oversized body can be buffered without the streaming cap.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed 64 MiB compatibility tradeoff; there is no narrow code defect for an automated repair worker.

target.title: fix(matrix): bound raw transport response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes unbounded raw body buffering in Matrix SDK transport when `maxBytes` is not provided by the caller.

**Root cause:** `performMatrixRequest` with `raw: true` falls through to `Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer())` when `params.maxBytes` is undefined. Any caller that omits `maxBytes` (e.g. `downloadContent(mxcUrl)` in `sdk.ts:532`) can buffer arbitrarily large media files — images, videos, encrypted attachments — exhausting process memory.

**Fix:** Use the existing `MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES` constant (already defined in the file) as a safe default upper bound. Remove the conditional split; all raw reads now go through `readResponseWithLimit`.

**Compatibility:** Callers that already pass `maxBytes` see no behavior change. Callers that omit it (previously unbounded) now get a `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` if the response exceeds `MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES`. This is fail-closed: the alternative is unbounded OOM.

## Validation Evidence

Local terminal output (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts --reporter=verbose`):

```
 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdc/.../openclaw

 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects oversized raw responses before buffering the whole body 29ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects malformed raw content-length before buffering the body 5ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > applies streaming byte limits when raw responses omit content-length 19ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > uses the matrix-specific idle-timeout error for stalled raw downloads 8ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > uses undici runtime fetch for pinned Matrix requests so the dispatcher stays bound 5ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects oversized JSON responses via content-length before buffering the body 3ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > applies streaming byte limits when JSON responses omit content-length 4ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > uses the JSON-specific idle-timeout error for stalled JSON downloads 3ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > rejects oversized raw responses when maxBytes is not provided (default MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES) 4ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > returns raw buffer bodies that stay under the default MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES limit 5ms
 ✓ performMatrixRequest > returns full JSON bodies that stay under the byte limit 2ms
 ✓ createMatrixGuardedFetch > rejects and cancels SDK responses above the declared size limit 2ms
 ✓ createMatrixGuardedFetch > strips matrix-js-sdk state_after sync opt-in from /sync requests 4ms
 ✓ createMatrixGuardedFetch > leaves non-sync Matrix requests unchanged 3ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
```

New tests added:
- Over-cap (Content-Length > MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES, `maxBytes` omitted): rejects with `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` before buffering ✓
- Under-cap (small body, `maxBytes` omitted): resolves to correct Buffer ✓

## Security & Privacy

No new network destinations. No credential handling. Narrows a memory-exhaustion path — responses that would previously buffer without limit now reject with `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` at 64 MiB.

## Compatibility

| Scenario | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `downloadContent(url)` with large media | OOM / process crash | Fail-closed `MatrixMediaSizeLimitError` |
| `downloadContent(url)` with normal media | Returns full Buffer | Returns full Buffer (unchanged) |
| Caller provides explicit `maxBytes` | Bounded | Bounded (unchanged) |
target.author: alix-007
